body:
## Source
Flagged URGENT in **Squad Tech News** Teams channel by Tamir (2026-03-16).

## Summary
Ingress-NGINX has reached **End of Life today (March 16, 2026)**. The guidance is to migrate to **Gateway API**.

## Action Items
- [ ] Audit all DK8S clusters for ingress-nginx usage
- [ ] Identify migration path to Gateway API
- [ ] Create migration timeline and plan
- [ ] Coordinate with DK8S team on rollout

## Priority
🔴 **URGENT** — EOL is effective today. Migration audits should start immediately.

---
*Created by Ralph (CPC-tamir-WCBED) from Teams monitoring at 2026-03-16T10:09:30Z*

# 🚨 Ingress-NGINX EOL Research & DK8S Migration Audit

**Researched by:** B'Elanna (Infrastructure Expert)
**Date:** 2026-03-16 (EOL DAY)
**Priority:** CRITICAL — EOL is TODAY

---

## 1. Current EOL Status — Facts

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| **What's retiring** | `kubernetes/ingress-nginx` — the community-maintained Ingress-NGINX controller |
| **EOL date** | **March 2026** (effective today, 2026-03-16) |
| **NOT affected** | F5/NGINX Inc's `nginxinc/kubernetes-ingress` (still actively maintained) |
| **Post-EOL impact** | No new releases, no bug fixes, no security patches. Repo goes read-only. |
| **Existing images** | Container images and Helm charts remain available but will NOT be updated |
| **Known CVEs** | CVE-2025-1974 (critical) — will NOT be patched post-EOL |
| **Compliance risk** | Running EOL software blocks SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS certifications |
| **Usage scope** | Used in ~40-50% of all Kubernetes clusters globally |

### Why It's Retiring
- Chronic maintainer shortage (1-2 people maintaining software used in half of K8s clusters)
- Significant accumulated technical debt
- Custom NGINX snippets became serious security attack vectors
- The planned successor (InGate) never matured
- Kubernetes networking innovation has shifted entirely to Gateway API

### Official Sources
- [Kubernetes Blog: Ingress NGINX Retirement](https://kubernetes.io/blog/2025/11/11/ingress-nginx-retirement/)
- [Kubernetes Steering & Security Statement (Jan 2026)](https://kubernetes.io/blog/2026/01/29/ingress-nginx-statement/)
- [Google Open Source Blog](https://opensource.googleblog.com/2026/02/the-end-of-an-era-transitioning-away-from-ingress-nginx.html)

---

## 2. Gateway API — The Recommended Migration Path

### What Is Gateway API?
The **Kubernetes Gateway API** is the official successor to the Ingress API. It provides a standardized, role-oriented API for advanced cloud-native networking.

### Key Resources
| Gateway API Resource | Purpose |
|---------------------|---------|
| `GatewayClass` | Defines the controller implementation (infra provider) |
| `Gateway` | Configures the listener (ports, protocols, TLS) — managed by cluster admins |
| `HTTPRoute` | Defines HTTP routing rules — managed by app teams |
| `TCPRoute` / `TLSRoute` / `GRPCRoute` | Protocol-specific routing |
| `ReferenceGrant` | Cross-namespace access control |

### Benefits Over Ingress-NGINX

| Capability | Ingress-NGINX | Gateway API |
|-----------|--------------|-------------|
| Role separation | ❌ Mixed infra/app config | ✅ Clear GatewayClass → Gateway → Route hierarchy |
| Advanced routing | ⚠️ Via annotations only | ✅ Native header/method/query-param routing |
| Traffic splitting | ⚠️ Annotation-based canary | ✅ Native weighted backends |
| Multi-protocol | ❌ HTTP/HTTPS only | ✅ HTTP, TCP, UDP, gRPC, WebSocket |
| Multi-tenancy | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Built-in cross-namespace with ReferenceGrant |
| Portability | ❌ NGINX-specific annotations | ✅ Vendor-agnostic spec |
| Policy attachment | ❌ Ad-hoc | ✅ Standardized PolicyAttachment pattern |
| Security model | ⚠️ Snippet injection risks | ✅ No arbitrary config injection |

### Recommended Gateway Implementations for DK8S
1. **NGINX Gateway Fabric** — F5/NGINX's Gateway API implementation (closest migration path)
2. **Envoy Gateway** — Lightweight, CNCF-backed, built on Envoy proxy
3. **Istio Gateway** — If already using Istio service mesh
4. **Traefik** — Full Gateway API support with additional features

---

## 3. DK8S Cluster Migration Audit Checklist

### Phase 0: Discovery & Assessment (Week 1)
- [ ] **Inventory all Ingress resources** across all DK8S clusters:
  ```bash
  kubectl get ingress --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | {namespace: .metadata.namespace, name: .metadata.name, host: .spec.rules[].host}'
  ```
- [ ] **Identify which controller** each cluster uses (`kubernetes/ingress-nginx` vs `nginxinc/kubernetes-ingress`)
- [ ] **Document all custom annotations** — especially NGINX-specific ones:
  ```bash
  kubectl get ingress --all-namespaces -o json | jq '[.items[].metadata.annotations // {} | keys[]] | unique'
  ```
- [ ] **Catalog custom NGINX snippets** (HIGH RISK — these have no Gateway API equivalent)
- [ ] **Map TLS certificate management** (cert-manager integration, secret references)
- [ ] **Identify ExternalDNS dependencies**
- [ ] **Document rate limiting, auth, and WAF configurations**
- [ ] **Check ConfigGen templates** for any Ingress resource generation

### Phase 1: Environment Preparation (Week 2)
- [ ] **Install Gateway API CRDs** on test clusters:
  ```bash
  kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.0/standard-install.yaml
  ```
- [ ] **Select and deploy Gateway implementation** (recommend NGINX Gateway Fabric for easiest migration path)
- [ ] **Verify CRD installation**: `kubectl get crd | grep gateway`
- [ ] **Create GatewayClass resource** for selected implementation
- [ ] **Update RBAC** — Gateway API has different permission model:
  - Infra admins → manage GatewayClass
  - Cluster admins → manage Gateway
  - App teams → manage HTTPRoute
- [ ] **Update Helm charts / ConfigGen templates** to generate Gateway API resources

### Phase 2: Conversion & Testing (Weeks 3-4)
- [ ] **Run `ingress2gateway` conversion tool** on existing manifests:
  ```bash
  # Install the tool
  go install github.com/kubernetes-sigs/ingress2gateway@latest
  # Convert
  ingress2gateway print --providers ingress-nginx --all-namespaces
  ```
- [ ] **Manual review** of all converted resources — especially:
  - TLS termination configuration
  - Path matching semantics (Ingress uses prefix by default; Gateway API requires explicit type)
  - Header-based routing rules
  - Backend weight distributions
- [ ] **Deploy Gateway resources in parallel** — both old and new get separate external IPs
- [ ] **Validate each route** against existing Ingress behavior
- [ ] **Test cert-manager integration** with Gateway API
- [ ] **Test ExternalDNS** with Gateway resources
- [ ] **Load test** the new Gateway setup against production-like traffic

### Phase 3: Staged Rollout (Weeks 5-6)
- [ ] **DNS-based traffic shifting**: Point a % of traffic to new Gateway IP
- [ ] **Monitor error rates, latency, and throughput** during parallel operation
- [ ] **Migrate non-critical services first**
- [ ] **Migrate critical services** with rollback plan ready
- [ ] **Validate monitoring/alerting** works with new resources

### Phase 4: Cutover & Cleanup (Week 7)
- [ ] **Full DNS cutover** to Gateway API endpoints
- [ ] **Remove Ingress-NGINX controller** Helm release
- [ ] **Delete legacy Ingress resources**
- [ ] **Remove old CRDs** if no longer needed
- [ ] **Update runbooks and documentation**
- [ ] **Update ConfigGen templates** to only generate Gateway API resources
- [ ] **Notify all service teams** of completed migration

---

## 4. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|------------|
| **Unpatched CVEs in production** | 🔴 Critical | Certain (post-EOL) | Migrate ASAP; no patches coming |
| **Compliance audit failure** | 🔴 Critical | High | Document migration plan for auditors NOW |
| **Custom NGINX snippets have no equivalent** | 🟠 High | Medium | Audit snippets; redesign using Gateway API policies or implementation-specific extensions |
| **Annotation incompatibility** | 🟡 Medium | High | Use `ingress2gateway` tool + manual review |
| **IP address change during migration** | 🟡 Medium | Certain | Parallel deployment → DNS cutover approach |
| **Traffic disruption during cutover** | 🟡 Medium | Low | Weighted DNS shifting; rollback plan |
| **Team unfamiliarity with Gateway API** | 🟡 Medium | Medium | Training sessions; start with non-critical services |
| **ConfigGen template updates needed** | 🟡 Medium | High | Coordinate with ConfigGen team for new resource templates |

---

## 5. Recommended Timeline

Given that **EOL is TODAY**, we are already in the danger zone. Here's the emergency timeline:

```
NOW → Week 1:  Phase 0 — Discovery & Assessment (STARTED)
Week 2:         Phase 1 — Environment Preparation
Weeks 3-4:      Phase 2 — Conversion & Testing
Weeks 5-6:      Phase 3 — Staged Rollout
Week 7:         Phase 4 — Cutover & Cleanup
Week 8:         Buffer / Post-migration validation
```

**Target completion: May 11, 2026** (8 weeks from today)

### Immediate Actions Required
1. ⚡ **TODAY**: Run the Ingress inventory commands on all DK8S clusters
2. ⚡ **THIS WEEK**: Identify which clusters use `kubernetes/ingress-nginx` vs alternatives
3. ⚡ **THIS WEEK**: File security advisory for compliance tracking
4. ⚡ **NEXT WEEK**: Begin Gateway API CRD installation on non-prod clusters

---

## References

- [Kubernetes Gateway API Docs](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/)
- [Official Migration Guide: Ingress to Gateway API](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/guides/getting-started/migrating-from-ingress-nginx/)
- [ingress2gateway Tool](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/ingress2gateway)
- [NGINX Gateway Fabric](https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric)
- [Microsoft Azure Architecture Blog: From Ingress to Gateway API](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azurearchitectureblog/from-ingress-to-gateway-api-a-pragmatic-path-forward-and-why-it-matters-now/4489779)

---

*If it ships, it ships reliably. This one stops shipping today — time to move.* — B'Elanna

### 📋 Assigned to B'Elanna (Infrastructure Expert)

**Issue:** #644 — [URGENT] Ingress-NGINX EOL (March 16) — Migrate to Gateway API

B'Elanna will pick this up in the next Copilot session.

> **For Copilot coding agent:** If enabled, this issue will be worked automatically.
> Otherwise, start a Copilot session and say:
> `B'Elanna, work on issue #644`
📋 Marked as `release:backlog` — assign a release target when ready.
🔄 Claimed by **TAMIRDRESHER** at 2026-03-16T11:26:35Z — Starting ingress-nginx EOL migration research and planning.

## Migration Plan Created

Comprehensive migration plan from ingress-nginx to Gateway API has been created at `research/ingress-nginx-eol-migration-plan.md`.

### Key Recommendations:
- **Use Envoy Gateway** as the primary Gateway API implementation for DK8S — full GA conformance, zero-disruption hot-reload via xDS, 1P alignment (AKS App GW for Containers is Envoy-based), native mTLS/rate-limiting/OTel support
- **Use ingress2gateway tool** for automated conversion of Ingress resources, with manual adjustments for snippet annotations and implementation-specific policies
- **Prioritize FedRAMP/government cloud clusters** for early migration — running EOL ingress-nginx is a P0 compliance blocker

### Proposed Timeline:
- **Week 1-2:** Audit all Ingress resources across DK8S clusters
- **Week 2-4:** Convert using ingress2gateway + manual policy creation
- **Week 4-6:** Parallel run (old + new side-by-side)
- **Week 6-7:** DNS cutover to new Gateway
- **Week 7-8:** Cleanup and close
- **Total: 8 weeks to full migration**

### Risk Mitigations:
- Rollback plan: DNS switch back to ingress-nginx LB (< 5 min)
- File compliance exception for migration window
- Keep ingress-nginx running parallel until 1 week stable on new gateway

Ready for review. /cc @tamirdresher_microsoft

## B'Elanna Follow-Up: Urgency Reassessment + Repo Audit + Gateway API Config Drafts

**Date:** 2026-03-16 (follow-up to initial research)
**Scope:** Deep-dive into DK8S-specific impact, repo audit, AKS extended support finding, and concrete config migration

---

### URGENCY DOWNGRADE: Plan the Migration, NOT Stop Everything

**Critical finding from Microsoft/AKS team:**

> Azure's **Application Routing add-on** (which uses ingress-nginx under the hood) will continue to receive **critical security patches through November 2026** — even though the upstream community project is EOL today.
> — [AKS Blog: Ingress-NGINX Update](https://blog.aks.azure.com/2025/11/13/ingress-nginx-update)

**What this means for DK8S:**
- If DK8S clusters use the AKS Application Routing add-on (managed NGINX), you have until **November 2026** for security patches
- If DK8S uses self-managed `kubernetes/ingress-nginx` Helm charts, you are **unpatched starting today**
- Either way, migration should complete well before November 2026

**Revised urgency: HIGH (plan and execute) — not CRITICAL (stop everything)**

The 8-week timeline from the earlier comments is still correct and appropriate.

---

### Repo Audit Results

Searched this repo (`tamresearch1`) for all ingress-related configs:

| Finding | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **`kind: Ingress` resources** | **None found** — no actual Ingress manifests in this repo |
| **`kind: Gateway` / `HTTPRoute`** | None yet |
| **NetworkPolicies for ingress-nginx namespace** | 3 files in `docs/fedramp/` (FedRAMP compensating controls from Issue #54) |
| **Helm template** | `docs/fedramp/helm-networkpolicy-template.yaml` — references ingress-nginx labels |
| **Helm values** | `docs/fedramp/helm-values-networkpolicy.yaml` — ingress-nginx namespace config |
| **FedRAMP Assessment** | `FEDRAMP_P0_NGINX_INGRESS_ASSESSMENT.md` — Worf's CVE assessment (Issue #51) |
| **DK8S Stability Runbook** | References ingress-nginx in Tier 1 runbook |

**Bottom line:** The actual Ingress resources and ingress-nginx controllers live in the DK8S platform clusters, not this repo. This repo has **supporting configs** (NetworkPolicies, Helm templates, runbooks) that need updating as part of the migration.

---

### Configs That Need Migration

#### 1. FedRAMP NetworkPolicies — Update Namespace References

The 3 NetworkPolicy files in `docs/fedramp/` target the `ingress-nginx` namespace. When migrating to Gateway API, these need namespace updates:

**Files to update:**
- `docs/fedramp/networkpolicy-ingress-default-deny.yaml` — change namespace to gateway controller namespace
- `docs/fedramp/networkpolicy-ingress-controller.yaml` — update pod selectors + namespace
- `docs/fedramp/networkpolicy-ingress-sovereign.yaml` — update pod selectors + namespace

**Key changes needed:**
- Namespace: `ingress-nginx` to `nginx-gateway` (or `envoy-gateway-system`)
- Pod selector labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx` to new controller labels
- Port changes: healthcheck port 10254 may differ; admission webhook port 8443 may change
- Add port for Gateway API webhook validation

#### 2. Helm Template — Update Labels and Selectors

`docs/fedramp/helm-networkpolicy-template.yaml` uses ingress-nginx label helpers. These need updating to the new gateway controller chart label helpers.

#### 3. DK8S Runbook — Update Procedures

The Tier 1 stability runbook references ingress-nginx troubleshooting steps. These need updating to Gateway API equivalents (different controller pods, different log locations, different metrics endpoints).

---

### Recommended Implementation: Envoy Gateway (Aligned with AKS)

Concurring with the earlier migration plan — **Envoy Gateway** is the right choice for DK8S:

| Factor | Envoy Gateway | NGINX Gateway Fabric |
|--------|--------------|---------------------|
| **AKS alignment** | AKS App GW for Containers is Envoy-based | Separate from AKS managed offering |
| **Gateway API conformance** | Full GA conformance | Full GA conformance |
| **Hot reload** | xDS-based (zero-disruption) | NGINX reload (brief connection drop) |
| **mTLS** | Native | Requires additional config |
| **Observability** | Native OTel support | Prometheus only |
| **Rate limiting** | Built-in global rate limiting | Requires separate component |

---

### Updated Action Items

| # | Action | Owner | Status | Deadline |
|---|--------|-------|--------|----------|
| 1 | Confirm whether DK8S uses AKS Application Routing add-on or self-managed ingress-nginx | Tamir / DK8S team | Pending | This week |
| 2 | If self-managed: immediately check if latest patched version (v1.13.7+) is deployed | B'Elanna | Blocked on #1 | This week |
| 3 | Update FedRAMP NetworkPolicy configs for new gateway namespace | B'Elanna | Pending | Week 2 |
| 4 | Update Helm NetworkPolicy template for new controller labels | B'Elanna | Pending | Week 2 |
| 5 | Update DK8S stability runbook gateway sections | B'Elanna | Pending | Week 3 |
| 6 | File compliance exception for migration window | Worf | Pending | This week |
| 7 | Review ConfigGen templates for Ingress resource generation | Data | Pending | Week 2 |
| 8 | Gateway API CRD install on non-prod clusters | B'Elanna / DK8S | Pending | Week 2 |

**Key question for Tamir:** Does DK8S use the **AKS Application Routing add-on** (managed by Azure, patches until Nov 2026) or **self-managed ingress-nginx Helm charts** (unpatched as of today)? This determines whether we have 8 weeks or 8 months of runway.

---

*Configs don't migrate themselves. Let's get the controller type confirmed and I'll start updating the FedRAMP policies.* — B'Elanna

title: [URGENT] Ingress-NGINX EOL (March 16) — Migrate to Gateway API
